title: Static Web Deployment
summary: Deploy HTML, CSS and JavaScript applications with safe configuration and repeatable releases.

lessons.title: Prepare the project structure
lessons.objective: Organise browser files so a static host can serve them predictably.
lessons.content: A static deployment needs a clear entry file, relative asset paths and no dependency on local file-system paths.

lessons.title: Protect secrets
lessons.objective: Recognise why browser code cannot safely contain private API keys.
lessons.content: Anything shipped to a browser can be inspected. Private keys must stay on a trusted server or serverless function.

lessons.title: Create a release
lessons.objective: Produce a version, release note and verification checklist.
lessons.content: A release should describe what changed, what was tested, known limitations and how to roll back.

title: Python Service Deployment
summary: Package and release a Python API or application with dependencies, configuration and health checks.

lessons.title: Define the entry point
lessons.objective: Make application startup explicit and reproducible.
lessons.content: A deployment platform needs a deterministic command that starts the application.

lessons.title: Lock dependencies
lessons.content: Production builds should not depend on packages that happen to be installed on a developer machine.

lessons.title: Add operational evidence
lessons.objective: Provide health checks, logs and failure visibility.
lessons.content: A service is not production-ready merely because it starts. Operators need evidence that it is healthy.

title: Java Service Deployment
summary: Package a Java service with build automation, configuration and operational checks.

lessons.title: Create a reproducible build
lessons.objective: Use a build tool to create a deployable artefact.
lessons.content: A production Java service should be built through Maven or Gradle rather than an IDE-only action.

lessons.title: Externalise configuration
lessons.objective: Separate environment-specific values from source code.
lessons.content: Database URLs, credentials and service endpoints should be supplied through controlled configuration.

lessons.title: Prove service health
lessons.content: A deployment pipeline needs a reliable way to distinguish successful startup from a broken release.

title: SQL Change Delivery
summary: Deliver SQL changes through controlled migrations, validation and rollback.

lessons.title: Version database changes
lessons.objective: Represent schema and data changes as ordered migrations.
lessons.content: Manual production edits are difficult to reproduce and audit. Versioned migration files create traceable change.

lessons.title: Validate before and after
lessons.objective: Create controls proving that the change preserved expected data.
lessons.content: A SQL release should include row-count, reconciliation and business-rule checks.

lessons.title: Plan rollback
lessons.objective: Define how to recover when a migration fails.
lessons.content: Some database changes cannot be safely reversed automatically. The release must state the recovery strategy.
